title: Puccinellia foucaudii
taxonomy: Puccinellia foucaudii is classified within the family Poaceae, commonly known as grasses. This classification is due to its morphological characteristics and shared traits with other members of this family. Within the Poaceae family, Puccinellia foucaudii is placed in the tribe Poeae. The genus Puccinellia is further grouped under the subfamily Pooideae. As a member of the Puccinellia genus, Puccinellia foucaudii is a type of salt-marsh grass.

side_effects: Puccinellia foucaudii, a plant native to the Mediterranean region, is not typically known for having any significant side effects when handled properly. intro: Puccinellia foucaudii, also known as Foucault's grass or sea puccinellia, is a type of flowering plant in the grass family that thrives in coastal and estuarine environments. Traditionally, Puccinellia foucaudii has been used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In herbal preparations, Puccinellia foucaudii is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to promote respiratory health and alleviate symptoms of coughs and colds.
